grammar.meaning: when it comes to (a high level/status)
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]経営者ともなると、システムを構築することが求められる。
grammar.translation: [ファイル5] When one becomes a 経営者, システムを構築する is required.
wrong_choice_explanations: ともなれば means 'conditional: once one reaches the level of'; it doesn't convey the 'when it comes to (a high level/status)' meaning needed here.
としても means 'even if considered as / even as'; it doesn't convey the 'when it comes to (a high level/status)' meaning needed here.
にしては means 'for / considering that (unexpectedness)'; it doesn't convey the 'when it comes to (a high level/status)' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: once one reaches the level of
grammar.example_sentence: 教授ともなれば、研究論文の発表が求められる。
grammar.translation: Once one reaches the rank of professor, publication of research papers is required.
wrong_choice_explanations: ともなると means 'when it comes to a high level/status'; it doesn't convey the 'once one reaches the level of' meaning needed here.
と言えば means 'when it comes to speaking of'; it doesn't convey the 'once one reaches the level of' meaning needed here.
のようになると means 'when one becomes like'; it doesn't convey the 'once one reaches the level of' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 困難をものともせず、プロジェクトを推進した。
grammar.translation: Undaunted by difficulties, we advanced the project.
wrong_choice_explanations: ともなれば means 'conditional: once one reaches the level of'; it doesn't convey the 'undaunted by / in defiance of' meaning needed here.
なりとも means 'some / any (even a small amount)'; it doesn't convey the 'undaunted by / in defiance of' meaning needed here.
ばかりか means 'let alone / far from'; it doesn't convey the 'undaunted by / in defiance of' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 社会福祉の充実んがため、予算が配分された。
grammar.translation: For the sake of improving social welfare, a budget was allocated.
wrong_choice_explanations: んばかりに means 'as if / almost as though'; it doesn't convey the 'in order to (literary)' meaning needed here.
ために means 'for / in order to (standard)'; it doesn't convey the 'in order to (literary)' meaning needed here.
ゆえに means 'because of / due to'; it doesn't convey the 'in order to (literary)' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: as if about to / almost as though
grammar.example_sentence: 泣かんばかりに、その報告に反応した。
grammar.translation: He reacted to the report almost as if about to cry.
wrong_choice_explanations: んがため means 'unknown form: んがため'; it doesn't convey the 'as if about to / almost as though' meaning needed here.
ために means 'for / in order to (standard)'; it doesn't convey the 'as if about to / almost as though' meaning needed here.
べく means 'in order to / so as to'; it doesn't convey the 'as if about to / almost as though' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: not even one / not a single
grammar.example_sentence: 一銭たりとも無駄にしてはならない。
grammar.translation: Not even a single cent should be wasted.
wrong_choice_explanations: ほかならぬ means 'nothing but / only'; it doesn't convey the 'not even one / not a single' meaning needed here.
までも means 'even / as far as'; it doesn't convey the 'not even one / not a single' meaning needed here.
すら means 'even (stronger emphasis)'; it doesn't convey the 'not even one / not a single' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 日本の文化ならでは の美しさが感じられる。
grammar.translation: The beauty unique to Japanese culture is felt.
wrong_choice_explanations: ばかりか means 'let alone / far from'; it doesn't convey the 'unique to / only possible with' meaning needed here.
ならではの means 'unique to (with noun modifier)'; it doesn't convey the 'unique to / only possible with' meaning needed here.
ならば means 'if / in case of'; it doesn't convey the 'unique to / only possible with' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: その提案は検討に耐える内容である。
grammar.translation: That proposal is worthy of examination.
wrong_choice_explanations: にたえない means 'cannot bear / unworthy of'; it doesn't convey the 'worthy of / can withstand' meaning needed here.
に値する means 'worthy of / deserves'; it doesn't convey the 'worthy of / can withstand' meaning needed here.
として means 'as / functioning as'; it doesn't convey the 'worthy of / can withstand' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: その行為は非難に耐えない。
grammar.translation: That action is unworthy of criticism / cannot bear criticism.
wrong_choice_explanations: にたえる means 'worthy of / can withstand'; it doesn't convey the 'cannot bear / unworthy of' meaning needed here.
に対する means 'towards / against'; it doesn't convey the 'cannot bear / unworthy of' meaning needed here.
にせよ means 'even if / whether or not'; it doesn't convey the 'cannot bear / unworthy of' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 医者まじき振る舞いだ。
grammar.translation: It is conduct unbecoming a doctor.
wrong_choice_explanations: ではあるまじき means 'should not be / unbecoming (with auxiliary)'; it doesn't convey the 'unbecoming of / should not (literary)' meaning needed here.
べきではない means 'should not / ought not'; it doesn't convey the 'unbecoming of / should not (literary)' meaning needed here.
ってはいけない means 'must not / should not'; it doesn't convey the 'unbecoming of / should not (literary)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 嘘をつくべからず。
grammar.translation: One must not lie.
wrong_choice_explanations: べからざる means 'that must not be'; it doesn't convey the 'must not / shall not (prohibitive, literary)' meaning needed here.
べきでない means 'should not'; it doesn't convey the 'must not / shall not (prohibitive, literary)' meaning needed here.
してはならない means 'unknown form: してはならない'; it doesn't convey the 'must not / shall not (prohibitive, literary)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 否定すべからざる事実である。
grammar.translation: It is an undeniable fact.
wrong_choice_explanations: べからず means 'must not (archaic)'; it doesn't convey the 'indispensable / that must not be' meaning needed here.
べきではない means 'should not / ought not'; it doesn't convey the 'indispensable / that must not be' meaning needed here.
なくてはならない means 'must have / cannot do without'; it doesn't convey the 'indispensable / that must not be' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: apart from / other than (no one else)
grammar.example_sentence: この仕事は彼をおいてない。
grammar.translation: No one but him can do this job.
wrong_choice_explanations: をおきて means 'unknown form: をおきて'; it doesn't convey the 'apart from / other than (no one else)' meaning needed here.
を除いて means 'except for / excluding'; it doesn't convey the 'apart from / other than (no one else)' meaning needed here.
ほかに means 'other than / besides'; it doesn't convey the 'apart from / other than (no one else)' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: it might be different if... but
grammar.example_sentence: 戦争ならいざしらず、平時にそれは許されない。
grammar.translation: In war it might be understandable, but in peacetime it is not permitted.
wrong_choice_explanations: ならば means 'if / in case of'; it doesn't convey the 'it might be different if... but' meaning needed here.
ならいざ means 'it might be (incomplete form)'; it doesn't convey the 'it might be different if... but' meaning needed here.
であれば means 'if it is (conditional)'; it doesn't convey the 'it might be different if... but' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 日本語はおろか、英語もできない。
grammar.translation: Far from Japanese, he cannot even speak English.
wrong_choice_explanations: をはじめ means 'starting with / including'; it doesn't convey the 'let alone / far from' meaning needed here.
ばかりか means 'let alone / far from'; it doesn't convey the 'let alone / far from' meaning needed here.
にもかかわらず means 'despite / in spite of'; it doesn't convey the 'let alone / far from' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: not only... but also (the latter even more so)
grammar.example_sentence: 結果もさることながら、過程が重要だ。
grammar.translation: Not only the result but the process is important.
wrong_choice_explanations: ばかりか means 'let alone / far from'; it doesn't convey the 'not only... but also (the latter even more so)' meaning needed here.
だけではなく means 'not only but also'; it doesn't convey the 'not only... but also (the latter even more so)' meaning needed here.
にもかかわらず means 'despite / in spite of'; it doesn't convey the 'not only... but also (the latter even more so)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 最後のテストに至っては、合格者がいなかった。
grammar.translation: As for the final test, there were no passing students.
wrong_choice_explanations: に至る means 'reach / lead to (process)'; it doesn't convey the 'as for (the extreme case)' meaning needed here.
に関しては means 'regarding / concerning'; it doesn't convey the 'as for (the extreme case)' meaning needed here.
については means 'about / concerning'; it doesn't convey the 'as for (the extreme case)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 完璧ではないまでも、十分な結果である。
grammar.translation: While not perfect, it is a sufficient result.
wrong_choice_explanations: ないままでも means 'while remaining not (unusual)'; it doesn't convey the 'even if not / at least' meaning needed here.
ないたりとも means 'not a single (rare form)'; it doesn't convey the 'even if not / at least' meaning needed here.
ないかぎり means 'as long as not / unless'; it doesn't convey the 'even if not / at least' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: 謝罪せずにはすまない。
grammar.translation: One cannot help but apologize.
wrong_choice_explanations: ずにはいられない means 'cannot help but / must'; it doesn't convey the 'cannot avoid doing / must do' meaning needed here.
ないではいられない means 'cannot help but'; it doesn't convey the 'cannot avoid doing / must do' meaning needed here.
にもかかわらず means 'despite / in spite of'; it doesn't convey the 'cannot avoid doing / must do' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: it is not impossible / there is some possibility
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル1]完璧ではないものでもない試験成績である。
grammar.translation: It is not a test score that is not imperfect (i.e., somewhat good).
wrong_choice_explanations: ではあり得ない means 'unknown form: ではあり得ない'; it doesn't convey the 'it is not impossible / there is some possibility' meaning needed here.
ないこともない means 'unknown form: ないこともない'; it doesn't convey the 'it is not impossible / there is some possibility' meaning needed here.
ないとは言えない means 'unknown form: ないとは言えない'; it doesn't convey the 'it is not impossible / there is some possibility' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはてからというものの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for てからというもの.
wrong_choice_explanations: とは言えない means 'cannot be said that / is not the case'; it doesn't convey the 'ever since' meaning needed here.
てからは means 'after (indicates completion then start of new action)'; it doesn't convey the 'ever since' meaning needed here.
た後は means 'after that'; it doesn't convey the 'ever since' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはを皮切りにの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for を皮切りに.
wrong_choice_explanations: を皮切りとして means 'starting with (slightly different marker)'; it doesn't convey the 'starting with / beginning from' meaning needed here.
の始まりに means 'at the beginning of'; it doesn't convey the 'starting with / beginning from' meaning needed here.
きっかけに means 'occasioned by / as a trigger for'; it doesn't convey the 'starting with / beginning from' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: to be forced to / compelled to
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはを余儀なくされるの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for を余儀なくされる.
wrong_choice_explanations: とは言えない means 'cannot be said that / is not the case'; it doesn't convey the 'to be forced to / compelled to' meaning needed here.
を強いられる means 'to be forced to'; it doesn't convey the 'to be forced to / compelled to' meaning needed here.
を避けられない means 'cannot be avoided'; it doesn't convey the 'to be forced to / compelled to' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これは極まりないの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for 極まりない.
wrong_choice_explanations: 極めて means 'extremely (adverb form, not pattern)'; it doesn't convey the 'extremely / the height of' meaning needed here.
はなはだしく means 'extremely / exceedingly'; it doesn't convey the 'extremely / the height of' meaning needed here.
きわめて means 'extremely (written differently)'; it doesn't convey the 'extremely / the height of' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはの至りの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for の至り.
wrong_choice_explanations: の致し方ない means 'unavoidable / nothing can be done about'; it doesn't convey the 'the utmost / the height of (emotion)' meaning needed here.
のようには means 'is not like'; it doesn't convey the 'the utmost / the height of (emotion)' meaning needed here.
の限り means 'to the limit / as far as'; it doesn't convey the 'the utmost / the height of (emotion)' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: the pinnacle of / the extreme of
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはの極みの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for の極み.
wrong_choice_explanations: の境地 means 'state of mind / realm'; it doesn't convey the 'the pinnacle of / the extreme of' meaning needed here.
の頂点 means 'the peak / summit'; it doesn't convey the 'the pinnacle of / the extreme of' meaning needed here.
の最たるもの means 'the most outstanding example of'; it doesn't convey the 'the pinnacle of / the extreme of' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはたるの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for たる.
wrong_choice_explanations: であろう means 'probably / would be'; it doesn't convey the 'as (a person of status) / being (formal)' meaning needed here.
としての means 'as / in the capacity of'; it doesn't convey the 'as (a person of status) / being (formal)' meaning needed here.
ほどではない means 'is not as ... as / not quite'; it doesn't convey the 'as (a person of status) / being (formal)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはあってのの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for あっての.
wrong_choice_explanations: があってこそ means 'only because / only thanks to'; it doesn't convey the 'only because of / thanks to' meaning needed here.
のおかげで means 'thanks to / because of'; it doesn't convey the 'only because of / thanks to' meaning needed here.
によって means 'by / by means of'; it doesn't convey the 'only because of / thanks to' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはをいいことにの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for をいいことに.
wrong_choice_explanations: を幸いに means 'unknown form: を幸いに'; it doesn't convey the 'taking advantage of' meaning needed here.
を口実に means 'unknown form: を口実に'; it doesn't convey the 'taking advantage of' meaning needed here.
を機会に means 'unknown form: を機会に'; it doesn't convey the 'taking advantage of' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはをよそにの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for をよそに.
wrong_choice_explanations: を気にせず means 'without minding / ignoring'; it doesn't convey the 'ignoring / paying no attention to' meaning needed here.
を無視して means 'ignoring / disregarding'; it doesn't convey the 'ignoring / paying no attention to' meaning needed here.
もかまわず means 'without caring / regardless of'; it doesn't convey the 'ignoring / paying no attention to' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはにかたくないの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for にかたくない.
wrong_choice_explanations: に乗じて means 'taking advantage of / seizing the opportunity'; it doesn't convey the 'not hard to (imagine, understand)' meaning needed here.
につけて means 'whenever / on the occasion of'; it doesn't convey the 'not hard to (imagine, understand)' meaning needed here.
にかこつけて means 'using as a pretext'; it doesn't convey the 'not hard to (imagine, understand)' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: using as a pretext / under the guise of
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはにかこつけての例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for にかこつけて.
wrong_choice_explanations: を問わず means 'regardless of / no matter'; it doesn't convey the 'using as a pretext / under the guise of' meaning needed here.
に関係なく means 'irrespective of'; it doesn't convey the 'using as a pretext / under the guise of' meaning needed here.
によらず means 'regardless of'; it doesn't convey the 'using as a pretext / under the guise of' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはいかんによらずの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for いかんによらず.
wrong_choice_explanations: によっては means 'depending on / if'; it doesn't convey the 'regardless of' meaning needed here.
次第では means 'depending on'; it doesn't convey the 'regardless of' meaning needed here.
か否かで means 'depending on whether or not'; it doesn't convey the 'regardless of' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはいかんでの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for いかんで.
wrong_choice_explanations: たところ means 'when done / as a result of doing'; it doesn't convey the 'depending on' meaning needed here.
たような means 'seemed like / looked like'; it doesn't convey the 'depending on' meaning needed here.
たことで means 'as a result of'; it doesn't convey the 'depending on' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: even if one does / no matter how much
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはたところでの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for たところで.
wrong_choice_explanations: か〜ないか means 'whether or not / either or'; it doesn't convey the 'even if one does / no matter how much' meaning needed here.
も〜も means 'both and'; it doesn't convey the 'even if one does / no matter how much' meaning needed here.
か〜もしれない means 'might or'; it doesn't convey the 'even if one does / no matter how much' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはようが〜まいがの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
wrong_choice_explanations: であろうが means 'whether it is or not'; it doesn't convey the 'whether one does or not' meaning needed here.
にせよ means 'even if / whether or not'; it doesn't convey the 'whether one does or not' meaning needed here.
ともあれ means 'in any case / at any rate'; it doesn't convey the 'whether one does or not' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはであれの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for であれ.
wrong_choice_explanations: なるなり means 'become or (unclear form)'; it doesn't convey the 'whether it be / even if' meaning needed here.
すぐに means 'immediately'; it doesn't convey the 'whether it be / even if' meaning needed here.
の際 means 'at the time of / when'; it doesn't convey the 'whether it be / even if' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはなりの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for なり.
wrong_choice_explanations: が早いか means 'no sooner'; it doesn't convey the 'as soon as / the moment' meaning needed here.
や否や means 'as soon as / the instant'; it doesn't convey the 'as soon as / the moment' meaning needed here.
と同時に means 'unknown form: と同時に'; it doesn't convey the 'as soon as / the moment' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはが早いかの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for が早いか.
wrong_choice_explanations: や否や means 'as soon as / the instant'; it doesn't convey the 'no sooner had... than' meaning needed here.
のようには means 'is not like'; it doesn't convey the 'no sooner had... than' meaning needed here.
そばから means 'as fast as / no sooner than'; it doesn't convey the 'no sooner had... than' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはや否やの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for や否や.
wrong_choice_explanations: そばから means 'as fast as / no sooner than'; it doesn't convey the 'as soon as / the instant' meaning needed here.
と同時に means 'unknown form: と同時に'; it doesn't convey the 'as soon as / the instant' meaning needed here.
と共に means 'together with'; it doesn't convey the 'as soon as / the instant' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: as fast as / no sooner than (repeated frustration)
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはそばからの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for そばから.
wrong_choice_explanations: なくて means 'without doing (informal)'; it doesn't convey the 'as fast as / no sooner than (repeated frustration)' meaning needed here.
ないで means 'without doing'; it doesn't convey the 'as fast as / no sooner than (repeated frustration)' meaning needed here.
できずに means 'unable to do'; it doesn't convey the 'as fast as / no sooner than (repeated frustration)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはなくしての例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for なくして.
wrong_choice_explanations: にひきかえ means 'in contrast to'; it doesn't convey the 'without' meaning needed here.
に比べ means 'compared to'; it doesn't convey the 'without' meaning needed here.
に対して means 'towards / against'; it doesn't convey the 'without' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはにひきかえの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for にひきかえ.
wrong_choice_explanations: に沿って means 'along / in accordance with'; it doesn't convey the 'in contrast to / unlike' meaning needed here.
に合わせて means 'in time with / synchronized with'; it doesn't convey the 'in contrast to / unlike' meaning needed here.
に従って means 'in accordance with / following'; it doesn't convey the 'in contrast to / unlike' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはに即しての例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for に即して.
wrong_choice_explanations: にもて means 'unclear/non-standard form'; it doesn't convey the 'in accordance with / based on (reality/facts)' meaning needed here.
にまして means 'more than'; it doesn't convey the 'in accordance with / based on (reality/facts)' meaning needed here.
以上に means 'more than / beyond'; it doesn't convey the 'in accordance with / based on (reality/facts)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはにもましての例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for にもまして.
wrong_choice_explanations: てやまない means 'unknown form: てやまない'; it doesn't convey the 'more than / even more than' meaning needed here.
ている means 'is doing / in state of'; it doesn't convey the 'more than / even more than' meaning needed here.
ておく means 'to do in advance / to leave done'; it doesn't convey the 'more than / even more than' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: sincerely / from the bottom of one's heart
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはてやまないの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for てやまない.
wrong_choice_explanations: とあったら means 'if it were the case that'; it doesn't convey the 'sincerely / from the bottom of one's heart' meaning needed here.
であるならば means 'if it is the case that'; it doesn't convey the 'sincerely / from the bottom of one's heart' meaning needed here.
とあれば means 'unknown form: とあれば'; it doesn't convey the 'sincerely / from the bottom of one's heart' meaning needed here.

grammar.meaning: if it is the case that
grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはとあればの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for とあれば.
wrong_choice_explanations: たのに means 'and yet / although (past)'; it doesn't convey the 'if it is the case that' meaning needed here.
たけれども means 'although / but (past)'; it doesn't convey the 'if it is the case that' meaning needed here.
たのだが means 'and yet / although it is'; it doesn't convey the 'if it is the case that' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはものをの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for ものを.
wrong_choice_explanations: ありさま means 'state / condition (noun)'; it doesn't convey the 'and yet / although (regret/frustration)' meaning needed here.
ぶり means 'manner / way (noun)'; it doesn't convey the 'and yet / although (regret/frustration)' meaning needed here.
有様 means 'state / condition (kanji form of ありさま)'; it doesn't convey the 'and yet / although (regret/frustration)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これは始末だの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for 始末だ.
wrong_choice_explanations: ずに means 'without doing'; it doesn't convey the 'end up (in an undesirable state)' meaning needed here.
なしで means 'without doing'; it doesn't convey the 'end up (in an undesirable state)' meaning needed here.
によらず means 'regardless of'; it doesn't convey the 'end up (in an undesirable state)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これは手前の例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for 手前.
wrong_choice_explanations: がし means 'unclear or old-fashioned form'; it doesn't convey the 'because of (social obligation/appearance)' meaning needed here.
なので means 'because / it is that'; it doesn't convey the 'because of (social obligation/appearance)' meaning needed here.
からか means 'is it because / what makes'; it doesn't convey the 'because of (social obligation/appearance)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはだにの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for だに.
wrong_choice_explanations: さえ means 'even (less emphasis than すら)'; it doesn't convey the 'even just (thinking about it)' meaning needed here.
までも means 'even / as far as'; it doesn't convey the 'even just (thinking about it)' meaning needed here.
ですら means 'even / surprisingly (formal)'; it doesn't convey the 'even just (thinking about it)' meaning needed here.

grammar.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはすらの例文です。
grammar.translation: This is an example sentence for すら.
wrong_choice_explanations: をだけ means 'only (particle misuse)'; it doesn't convey the 'even (emphasis, stronger than さえ)' meaning needed here.
